reasoning: The document exhibits near-total content loss of its primary visual and textual data; the conversion failed to capture a central map, its legend, and over 100 county names. Although programmatic scores show a word count ratio of 1.0, this is a false positive caused by a baseline extraction error that only identified 4 words, whereas the LLM identified significant textual content embedded in the graphic. A visual score of 0.5 further confirms the catastrophic loss of the document's primary information.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The output fails to capture a significant amount of text content from the source. Specifically, the legend which provides the critical mapping for 'Phase 1 (2020)', 'Phase 2 (2021)', etc., is entirely missing from the textual elements. Additionally, over 100 county names displayed on the map, the status date, and the footer metadata (scale, spatial reference) are omitted from the machine-readable text. The output word count of 4 represents a loss of over 90% of the textual information present in
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Legend text (Phase 1 (2020), Phase 2 (2021), Phase 3 (2022), Phase 4 (2023), Not Started (2010 Data), In Progress, Initial QC Complete, SFHA Update, Needs Review); County names (e.g., Cherokee, Clay, Macon...); Footer metadata (Scale: 1:3,179,372, Spatial Reference Name, etc.); Status date (Status as of: 6/16/2025).
concerns.output_quote: The text elements only contain the document title: 'Building Footprint Update Status'. All legend and map-specific text is missing from the elements array.

concerns.analysis: The source PDF is organized around a large, central map showing the update status across North Carolina. The HTML output completely omits this central map, rendering only the document title and a logo that was originally in the footer of the source document. The visual organization is fundamentally broken because the primary content of the page (the map and its legend) is missing, leaving only a caption and an unrelated logo in its place.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: A full-page layout featuring a large map of North Carolina titled 'Risk Building Footprint Refresh' with a legend, scale, and multiple agency logos.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML displays the main title 'Building Footprint Update Status' followed by a single 'EMAP Accredited' logo and a caption 'Risk Building Footprint Refresh'. The entire map and its associated spatial context are gone.

concerns.analysis: The most significant meaningful image in the source PDF—the 'Risk Building Footprint Refresh' map—is completely missing from the HTML output. Additionally, the agency logos (DPS header logo, NC DPS footer logo, and NC Emergency Management logo) are also missing, with the exception of the EMAP logo which has been misplaced. The map, legend, north arrow, and scale bar are critical visual information that did not transition to the HTML.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The central map image showing North Carolina counties colored by status, including the legend, north arrow, and scale bar.
concerns.output_quote: No map image or legend is present in the HTML screenshot.
concerns.location: Page 1, central and bottom content areas

action_items: Manually extract and include the legend text and Phase information
Provide an accessible alternative, such as a table or detailed text description, for the county-level data visualized in the map
Ensure agency logos and footer metadata are correctly captured or alt-tagged
